pages.title: Best Skills for Code Review
pages.description: Five strong picks for teams that want better PR feedback, safer merges, and more disciplined review workflows.

skills.name: superpowers
skills.why_recommended: Best overall pick for engineering teams that want repeatable review workflows, not just ad hoc prompts. It is strong on planning, review loops, and execution discipline.

skills.name: Trail of Bits Security Skills
skills.why_recommended: Best for code review when security matters. It is useful for static analysis, code auditing, and surfacing higher-signal security concerns before merge.

skills.name: claude-code-agents
skills.why_recommended: Good fit when review is part of a broader delivery flow that includes validation, QA, and multiple agent roles.

skills.name: compound-engineering-plugin
skills.why_recommended: Strong choice for teams that want review quality to improve over time through shared lessons, not just one-off comments.

skills.name: claude-codex-settings
skills.why_recommended: Useful if your code review work spans GitHub, cloud integrations, and service-heavy workflows where context matters as much as the code diff.

pages.title: Best Skills for Security
pages.description: Five skills worth looking at first when the main question is not just whether something works, but whether it is safe enough to trust.

skills.name: Trail of Bits Security Skills
skills.why_recommended: The clearest security-first option in the catalog. Best for real audits, static analysis, and vulnerability-oriented review.

skills.name: skill-scanner
skills.why_recommended: Strong early-pass scanner when you need to triage unknown skills fast and decide what deserves manual review next.

skills.name: parry
skills.why_recommended: Helpful if you care about prompt injection, unsafe hook inputs, and agent pipelines that touch untrusted content.

skills.name: Dippy
skills.why_recommended: Worth considering if you want safer command approval flows instead of turning safety off to speed up automation.

skills.name: AgentSys
skills.why_recommended: Useful to study when you need to understand the real risk surface of a powerful automation system before enabling it widely.

pages.title: Best Skills for Productivity
pages.description: Five skills that make agent-assisted work more practical day to day by reducing friction, preserving context, or speeding up common workflows.

skills.name: superpowers
skills.why_recommended: Best overall productivity pick because it improves how you think, plan, review, and execute across real software work.

skills.name: planning-with-files
skills.why_recommended: Excellent if your agent work is getting complex and you need persistent plans, checkpoints, and visible task state.

skills.name: obsidian-skills
skills.why_recommended: Strong fit for users who want their research, notes, and agent outputs to stay useful after the session ends.

skills.name: claude-tmux
skills.why_recommended: Great for power users juggling multiple Claude Code sessions who want better switching and visibility.

skills.name: Dippy
skills.why_recommended: A practical productivity upgrade for users slowed down by constant command approval friction.

pages.title: Best Skills for Data
pages.description: Five skills for users who care about extraction, analysis, structured outputs, and turning messy information into usable decisions.

skills.name: claude-scientific-skills
skills.why_recommended: Best broad data/research pick because it spans science, analysis, engineering, finance, and structured thinking workflows.

skills.name: xlsx
skills.why_recommended: Best official option if your work involves spreadsheets, formulas, tabular analysis, and data-heavy office workflows.

skills.name: pdf
skills.why_recommended: Useful when the real bottleneck is extracting data and structure from PDFs rather than from code or APIs.

skills.name: read-only-postgres
skills.why_recommended: A good fit for database exploration and safe query workflows when you want analysis without write risk.

skills.name: obsidian-skills
skills.why_recommended: Useful for turning extracted or analyzed data into reusable notes, summaries, and connected knowledge.

pages.title: Best Skills for DevOps
pages.description: Five high-signal picks for infra, deployment, cloud operations, and environment-heavy workflows where mistakes are costly.

skills.name: cc-devops-skills
skills.why_recommended: Best direct fit for deployment, infrastructure, and DevOps-heavy workflows. It is one of the clearest infra-oriented packs in the ecosystem.

skills.name: compound-engineering-plugin
skills.why_recommended: Strong when DevOps work needs operational discipline, shared lessons, and cleaner engineering handoffs.

skills.name: AgentSys
skills.why_recommended: Powerful choice for workflow automation across production-style engineering tasks, reviews, and investigations.

skills.name: claude-codex-settings
skills.why_recommended: Good bridge option when your DevOps workflows span GitHub, Azure, databases, and service-heavy tooling.

skills.name: mcp-builder
skills.why_recommended: Useful for teams building operational integrations and internal tool connectivity rather than only using existing skills.
